_note: ABRAHAM CYCLE part 2. Beat 14 War of Kings & Melchizedek (Gen 14): Lot taken when Sodom falls; Abram's 318 rescue; Melchizedik king/priest of Salem blesses with bread and wine (ember -> Christ, priest forever, Ps 110/Heb 7); Abram tithes and refuses Sodom's spoils. Beat 15 Covenant of the Stars (Gen 15): 'I am your shield'; count the stars; 'he believed the LORD and it was credited as righteousness' (the gospel-in-Genesis, Rom 4/Gal 3); the smoking firepot covenant + the 400-years-in-Egypt foreshadow. Beat 16 Hagar & Ishmael (Gen 16): Sarai's shortcut; Hagar flees; the angel at the spring, 'El Roi, the God who sees me'; Ishmael born (Abram 86). Beat 17 Covenant of Circumcision (Gen 17): El Shaddai; Abram->Abraham, Sarai->Sarah; the sign; Abraham laughs; Isaac ('laughter') named. Extra banter per user request. Verified against Gen 14-17 (angels get NO profile, per policy).

posts.author: CHAR_673
posts.body: Word off the eastern road, and it is bad. Four kings out of the east came down and crushed the whole valley. Sodom and Gomorrah broke and ran. The kings stripped the cities of everything, food, goods, people, and turned back east with a train of captives a mile long. And here is the part that reaches us: Lot was living in Sodom. They took him too. Abram's own nephew, roped in with the rest of the plunder, marching east.

comments.author: CHAR_669
comments.body: I passed that column at a distance. Never seen so many people driven like cattle. Somebody has to tell Abram.

comments.author: CHAR_672
comments.body: Somebody already has. I have never in my life seen the old man move that fast.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: They have taken Lot. My brother's son, the boy I raised as near my own, hauled off east in chains. I am not a soldier. I am an old man with tents and flocks. But I have three hundred and eighteen men born in my own house, trained and trusted, and tonight every one of them is arming. We ride at dark and we do not stop until we bring him home. Family is not a thing you leave on the road.
posts.highlight: Abram arms his 318 to rescue the captured Lot (Gen 14:13-14)

comments.author: CHAR_052
comments.body: You gave that boy the better half of the whole land and he moved to the worst city in it, and now you are riding through the night to save him. I married a fool. The best fool God ever made. Come back to me, Abram.

comments.author: CHAR_115
comments.body: Born in your house, master, and I will die in your service if that is tonight. The three hundred and eighteen are ready. Give the word.

comments.author: CHAR_049
comments.body: @CHAR_052 I always come back to you. That was the deal.

posts.author: CHAR_667
posts.body: I have herded goats my whole life. Last night I was a soldier. We caught them near Dan in the dark, split into companies the way the master laid it out, and fell on them from every side at once. It was chaos and terror and over faster than I can tell it. We chased them half the night and we got everyone back. Lot. The women. The children. Every last cart of stolen goods. Three hundred and eighteen shepherds against the armies of four kings, and we are riding home in the sunrise. Do not tell me God is not with that man.
posts.react_note: Mavah, Hodael the Elder and many others

comments.author: CHAR_677
comments.body: My husband left a herdsman and came home a war hero and he has NOT stopped talking about it. Someone please take the sword away before he hurts himself with it.

comments.author: CHAR_667
comments.body: @CHAR_677 I will have you know I was very brave and only slightly lost for most of it.

posts.author: CHAR_116
posts.body: Abram came home victorious, and I went out to meet him with bread and wine. I am Melchizedek, king of Salem, and I serve God Most High, Maker of heaven and earth. I blessed him there on the road: Blessed be Abram of God Most High, and blessed be God Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand. He gave me a tenth of everything he had won. Not to me. To the One I serve. A man who wins a battle and gives the glory away has already won a far greater one.
posts.highlight: Melchizedek, king of Salem and priest of God Most High, blesses Abram with bread and wine; Abram tithes (Gen 14:18-20)
posts.ember: a priest-king who appears without genealogy, greater than Abram; the pattern of the eternal priest, Christ (Ps 110:4, Heb 7); bread and wine on the road home

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: The king of Sodom rode up and made me an offer: give me back my people, and keep all the goods for yourself. A fortune, free and clear. I told him no. I have raised my hand to the LORD, God Most High, Maker of heaven and earth, and I have sworn I will not take so much as a thread or the strap of a sandal from you. I will not have the king of Sodom saying for the rest of his days that HE is the one who made Abram rich. My God does that. Only my God. Give my nephew his things and we are done here.
posts.highlight: Abram refuses the king of Sodom's spoils: no one but God will get the credit for blessing him (Gen 14:21-24)
posts.ember: the man who would not grasp at Babel will not be enriched by Sodom either; his wealth must be God's gift alone

comments.author: CHAR_053
comments.body: You came for me through the whole night and then would not take a single coin for it. I do not deserve you, uncle. I am going to be better. I mean it this time.

comments.author: CHAR_674
comments.body: Turned down a king's fortune before supper. Just another day in this household. I love working here.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: The word of the LORD came to me tonight in a vision: Do not be afraid, Abram. I am your shield, and your very great reward. And I said back to Him what I have not let myself say out loud in years. Lord GOD, what will You even give me? I go on childless. The one who will inherit my house is Eliezer, my steward. You have given me everything but the one thing. A reward means nothing to a man with no son to leave it to.
posts.highlight: 'I am your shield, your very great reward'; Abram's honest lament that he is still childless (Gen 15:1-3)

comments.author: CHAR_115
comments.body: Master, I would have served your son with my whole heart. I never once wanted the inheritance. I wanted the boy. We have all wanted the boy. Keep asking Him.

comments.author: CHAR_049
comments.body: @CHAR_115 you are a better man than I deserve, Eliezer. He is not finished with me yet. He said so.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: He took me outside. He said: Look up at the sky, Abram, and count the stars, if you are able to count them. Then He said: So shall your offspring be. An old man standing in the cold under more stars than there are grains of sand, being told every one of them is a promise with my name on it. I did not argue. I did not ask how. I just believed Him. And the Scripture will say of that moment for the rest of time: he believed the LORD, and He credited it to him as righteousness.
posts.highlight: 'count the stars... so shall your offspring be'; Abram believed God, and it was credited as righteousness (Gen 15:5-6)
posts.ember: the hinge of the whole Bible: righteousness by faith, not by works (Rom 4:3, Gal 3:6); the many nations promised while the man is still childless

comments.author: CHAR_052
comments.body: You came back into the tent and just held me and would not say why, only that we should go outside and look up. So we stood there and counted stars like two children until we lost count. I do not fully understand it, husband. But I believe you believe it. And tonight that is enough for me.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: He made a covenant with me today, and I will never forget the strangeness and weight of it. He had me set out the pieces of the offering in two rows. Then as the sun went down a deep and dreadful sleep fell on me, and a thick and terrible darkness, and He told me hard things: my offspring would be strangers in a land not their own, enslaved and mistreated four hundred years, and then He would judge that nation and bring them out with great possessions. And then, in the dark, a smoking firepot and a blazing torch passed between the pieces, alone. I did not walk through. Only He did. He bound Himself to this. Not me. Him.
posts.highlight: the covenant sealed by God alone passing between the pieces; the 400 years in Egypt foretold (Gen 15:7-21)
posts.ember: God binds Himself to the covenant unilaterally; the Egypt bondage + Exodus foreshadowed centuries early

posts.author: CHAR_052
posts.body: I have waited on this promise until my patience wore straight through, and today I did something about it. The LORD has kept me from bearing children, so I told Abram to take Hagar, my Egyptian maid. If I cannot build a family, perhaps I can build one through her. He agreed. It is done. I am telling myself this is faith, finding a way to help God keep His word. Somewhere underneath I know it is the opposite of faith. But I am so tired of waiting. You have no idea how tired.
posts.highlight: Sarai gives Hagar to Abram, a human shortcut around the promise (Gen 16:1-3)
posts.ember: running ahead of God; the ache of a promise delayed drives Sarai to 'help' in a way that will cost everyone

comments.author: CHAR_675
comments.body: Nobody in this camp will say a word against you for it. We have all watched you carry this. But oh, my friend, I have a bad feeling in my bones about this one.

posts.author: CHAR_113
posts.body: I am with child, and everything in this camp has changed. I will be honest, because I have nothing else that is my own: when I knew I had conceived and my mistress had not, I looked at her differently. I could not help it. And she felt it, and she came down on me so hard I could not stay. So I ran. I am out in the wilderness now, on the road toward Shur, alone, carrying a child, with no plan past the next spring of water. This is what I am worth to them. A womb, and then a problem.
posts.highlight: tension between Hagar and Sarai; Hagar flees into the wilderness (Gen 16:4-6)

posts.author: CHAR_113
posts.body: I sat down by a spring in the desert, ready to be forgotten, and the Angel of the LORD found me there. Me. A runaway slave girl with nothing. He called me by my name and by my place, Hagar, servant of Sarai, and He told me to go back, and He gave me a promise of my own: my son will be called Ishmael, which means God hears, because the LORD has heard my misery. And I gave God a name back, the only one that fit. El Roi. The God who sees me. All my life I have been the one nobody looked at twice. And the God of heaven turned and saw me in a desert. I am going home. Not because they deserve me. Because He found me.
posts.highlight: the Angel of the LORD meets Hagar; Ishmael named; Hagar calls Him El Roi, 'the God who sees me' (Gen 16:7-14)
posts.ember: God sees and hears the outcast and the slave; the first person in Scripture to give God a name is a foreign servant woman

comments.author: CHAR_671
comments.body: Hagar. My own countrywoman, seen by the God of these Hebrews out in the sand. I do not have words. Come home safe.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: I have a son. Hagar bore him and I named him Ishmael, God hears, just as she was told. I am eighty-six years old and I am holding a boy of my own at last, and my heart does not know how to sort out everything it is feeling. Joy, all of it real. And underneath, a quiet question I cannot shake, one only God can answer: is this the son of the promise, or is this me, once again, trying to help Him along? I love this child completely. I just do not yet know what he is for.
posts.ember: Ishmael born (Abram 86); a true son, but the promise-son is still unspoken; the ache of a self-made answer

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: I am ninety-nine years old, and thirteen years have passed since I last heard clearly from Him, and this morning God appeared to me again. I am God Almighty, He said, El Shaddai. Walk before Me and be blameless. And He remade the whole covenant, bigger than before, and He did something I did not expect: He changed my name. No longer Abram, exalted father. From now on, Abraham. Father of a multitude of nations. He has renamed me for a future I still cannot see. A childless old man walking around called father of nations. Let them laugh. He said it, so it is as good as done.
posts.highlight: God Almighty (El Shaddai) renews the covenant; Abram becomes Abraham, father of many nations (Gen 17:1-8)
posts.ember: the new name given by God (contrast Babel's self-made name); 'father of many nations' spoken over a childless man

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: God gave the covenant a sign, and it is one no man would have chosen for himself. Every male among us is to be circumcised, cut in the flesh, an eighth-day mark on every generation after us that says: we belong to Him, down to the body, down to the line that carries the promise. Today it was done, to me at ninety-nine, to Ishmael, to every man born in my house and every servant. If we are going to be marked as His, let it be somewhere we cannot forget and cannot show off. This one is between us and God.
posts.react_note: Ishmael, Hodael the Elder and many others
posts.highlight: circumcision given as the sign of the covenant for every male of the household (Gen 17:9-14, 23-27)

comments.author: CHAR_674
comments.body: Master. I have followed you out of Ur, into Egypt, through a war against four kings, and I never once complained. I am complaining now. At MY age. This is a lot to ask.

comments.author: CHAR_049
comments.body: @CHAR_674 I am ninety-nine, old friend. Whatever you are about to say, I went first.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: He was not finished. As for Sarai, He said, her name will be Sarah, princess, for she will be the mother of nations and kings will come from her. My Sarah. And then the thing I have begged for across twenty-five years: she herself will bear you a son, and you will call him Isaac. I confess what I did. I fell on my face, and I laughed. A hundred-year-old man and a ninety-year-old woman, with a baby? I laughed, half in wonder and half in disbelief. So He took even that and folded it into the promise. Isaac. It means he laughs. God is going to have the last laugh, and He is naming the boy after it.
posts.highlight: Sarai becomes Sarah; God promises she will bear Isaac ('laughter'); Abraham laughs (Gen 17:15-19)
posts.ember: the son of the promise named and dated at last; Sarah, mother of nations and kings, the line to David and to Christ

comments.author: CHAR_052
comments.body: So you fell down laughing at the idea of me having a baby. We are going to have a long conversation about that, husband. A long one. ...but a princess, though. He called me a princess. I am keeping that.

comments.author: CHAR_049
comments.body: @CHAR_052 I laughed because it is impossible. I believe it because He is God. Both are true, princess.
